Group.Name: MultiStyle Labs
Group.Trivia: Nothing but a bunch of friends annoying you in their spare time.
Group.Slogan: we ar moor inteligend then yu

Handle.AKA: Superlinus, Bruce Leenus, Bruce Leet, Helmut Körschgen, Nihil Baxter, Sam Peckinpah

Scener.Trivia:
The only known man in Kleve who is allowed to push Fanta around.
SID-Remixes: [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6992 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6804 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6548 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6419 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6453 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6401 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6280 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6499 [url]https://remix.kwed.org/remix/6479
Spotify: [url]https://open.spotify.com/artist/4RUTqRhSBV9gadOLqp7HUX
Bandcamp: [url]http://www.mini-melodies.de

Handle.HandleStory: I made a typo once I coded some junk and I liked the "handle" and 6581 was added later on, since it responds the SID soundchip. ...known to like sisters :)

Scener.Trivia:
Most recognized for my HQ SID Recordings. Recorded from real hardware. http://www.youtube.com/demoscenes
Involved in "Preservation", to preserve the demoscene and unreleased releases for the future generation of data-sceners, basically to pass that time-capsule and legacy. Where other quiet, that is where I begin to tackle the hard-cases of floppies and tapes. To bring out as much data as possible! "The challenge is always accepted"
http://sidpreservation.6581.org -Read about all the work and what is going on-
Fulltime musician: http://www.soundcloud.com/dees-productions

Handle.HandleStory: For a time I only used xiny, as came up from a typo I made while coding but the "nick" had a nice touch so I dropped my old nick and started to use this one instead and the numbers 6581, well they are from the SID chip, since I am a musican. End of story :D

UserComment.Text: The man who smoke the SID and all that jazz <3 Such a brilliant tune! Bold bitch in the compo too!
Such great composition, harmony changes, bassline… wow! One of my top favorites at the compo. Also love the reverse-reverb-ish effects from 1:50 etc. Will listen to this one a lot in the future.

Scener.Trivia:
*** All of my tunes are SID8580 only. If they do anything on a SID6581 chip, it's a mere coincidence. ***
As a C64 composer I was born in 1989, died in 1994 and re-born in 2009 because of C64 remix album "rakBIT".
For everything, big thanks to all my friends. You know who you are and I do, too!
___Albums and side projects___
- NECROLEPSY (2025): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/necrolepsy-deluxe-edition
- SCOPEKILLERS compilation (2022): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/scopekillers-compilation
- DESTRONNAUTH (2019): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/destronnauth
- HYPERGAME (2017): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/hypergame
- AENTHERA (2016): https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/aenthera
- Bleep and Destroy (2014) by StrayWorx: https://strayboom.bandcamp.com/album/bleep-and-destroy
___C64 music collections___
- Darth SIDe: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/darth-side
- V8bit: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/v8bit
- Zeros as Ones: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/zeros-as-ones
- Voyage Eclectique: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/voyage-eclectique
___SID remix releases___
- Re:ReLive (2024) by SIDRIP Alliance: https://sidrip.bandcamp.com/album/re-relive
- ReLive (2015) by SIDRIP Alliance: https://sidrip.bandcamp.com/releases
- Johannes Bjerregaard tribute album: https://johannesbjerregaard.bandcamp.com/album/jb
- rakBIT by NP: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/rakbit
- Dark Flow by NP: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/album/dark-flow-the-expanding-ep
***
Youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/user/NecroPolo8580/videos
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/NecroPolo-138733566144931/?ref=tn_tnmn
Remix64: http://www.remix64.com/member/necropolo/
BandCamp: https://necropolo.bandcamp.com/music

Handle.HandleStory: An another old handle that I used when I started to make SIDs for games. None became released and some time after the developer's house burned down during the development of Geister in 1994 with all the sources I stopped making C64 music.
My very first scene handle that brought me an ample of finished tunes that I made for stuff that folks did not finish at the end in the early '90s.

UserComment.Text:
Completely owning the scene in the harmony change / jazz department. I love the fact that it is a statement of the genre without wanting to be a compo tune. Surely it will get a regular play for me.
Apart from we said hello and some regular small talk on the party, I missed the opportunity to ask questions and some deeper talk about music unfortunately. There are lots of interesting details going on in your tunes I'd like to ask about - but I'll do it next time. Keep being awesome, mate (of course you will).
Absolutely bossed the chords game in the compo with this head-bobber, no doubt about that. Very slick, very Linosh, thank you very much. <3
Love that uplifting harmony change from 1:51 - goosebumps! Classic jazzy Linus <3
To me this is THE perfect tune. Each instrument, each note is perfection.
Sophisticated and exquisite, a bold choice for a compo!
The visualizer was chosen with care :D
Lovely! I dig the ghost notes in the bass line, makes it sound human-played :D
I love how almost everything on the melody voice visualizer looks like middle fingers *lol*

Handle.HandleStory: My finger is an electron gun.

UserComment.Text: Lovely chill tune from Linus. Makes me warm and fuzzy inside!
One of my favourite entries in the compo. Should've ranked much higher, but I guess it's not suited for the common sceners. =)

Handle.HandleStory:
I'm a Scener since I've began to make SID's tunes in 2020. My first scene contact was LMan. I'm a digital music producer from north germany.
I've made a lot of covers and more will follow but I also write original tunes.
https://www.youtube.com/c/Nordischsound https://linktr.ee/Nordischsound https://www.facebook.com/Nordischsound

UserComment.Text: Perfekt harmony of dissonance, i like it, very unique sound :)

Handle.HandleStory: I have no idea how I ended up with such a horrible handle. Guess I had a hard time thinking one hehe.
